title: Alkermes — Projects
html:
Alkermes Project Portfolio

Governing Thought

The Alkermes project portfolio is compact: three active engagements (Lybalvi PET, Vivitrol PET, Aristada SOV) plus one pending proposal (SFTO). All three active projects are promotional effectiveness research — ZoomRx's core competency but also its ceiling on the account. The Lybalvi PET is the strategically important engagement: it now tracks Cobenfy alongside traditional competitors, making it the single best source of intelligence on how the first new-mechanism schizophrenia drug in 35 years is affecting the promotional landscape. The Aristada SOV Tracker is the longest-running engagement (5+ years) and the institutional anchor. The Vivitrol PET is likely in its final year given generic entry in January 2027. No projects exist for LUMRYZ (acquired Feb 2026) — this is the most significant gap.

Account Health Dashboard

# Project Brand Service Status Key Dynamic
1 LYBALVI PET Lybalvi Multichannel PET 🟢 Active Now tracking Cobenfy SoV — highest-value intelligence stream
2 ARISTADA SOV Tracker Aristada SOV Tracker 🟢 Active 5+ year quarterly series; stable at ~20% SOV
3 VIVITROL PET Vivitrol PET 🟡 Active (at risk) Generic entry Jan 2027; likely final year
4 Sales Force Tactical Optimization Lybalvi SFTO ⚪ Proposed $89.5K; awaiting client decision

Legend: 🟢 Active/Healthy | 🟡 Active with risk | 🔵 Recently delivered | ⚪ Proposed/Pipeline | 🔴 At risk/Stalled

Detailed Workstream Profiles

PET / Promotional Effectiveness Tracking

1. LYBALVI Multichannel PET ($338,701)

Status: 🟢 Active SOW value: $338,701 Scope: 2 full waves + 2 partial waves Signatory: John Fritch (VP, DAI)

What it tracks:

  • Share of Voice vs. Vraylar (cariprazine, AbbVie), Caplyta (lumateperone, Intra-Cellular), Rexulti (brexpiprazole, Otsuka/Lundbeck)
  • Cobenfy (xanomeline/trospium, BMS) reach/frequency/SoV — added post-approval
  • Sample-related questions (monitoring Cobenfy sample distribution)
  • Multichannel promotional mix (in-person, digital, peer-to-peer)
  • Prescriber segment analysis (schizophrenia vs. bipolar I prescribers)

Key findings to date:

  • [Populate from latest wave deliverable]
  • Cobenfy SoV tracking initiated — first wave results expected Q2 2025

Why it matters: This is the most strategically valuable project on the account. Cobenfy is the first new-mechanism schizophrenia drug in 35 years, and Alkermes needs real-time intelligence on how its promotional presence is affecting Lybalvi's competitive position. No other vendor is providing this.

Follow-up opportunities:

  • Deep-dive Cobenfy competitive study (beyond PET-level tracking)
  • Lybalvi bipolar I positioning study (if Cobenfy BPD trials advance)
  • Sales Force Tactical Optimization ($89.5K proposed — natural extension)

2. VIVITROL PET ($117,435)

Status: 🟡 Active (at risk for non-renewal) SOW value: $117,435 Scope: 1 wave, 100 interactions Signatory: Kurt Stauble (Sr. Dir., MR & CI)

What it tracks:

  • VIVITROL vs. SUBLOCADE (buprenorphine SC, Indivior) and BRIXADI (buprenorphine SC, Braeburn/Camurus)
  • Unaided awareness, message recall, rep quality
  • HCP perceptions on cost/efficacy/safety/access
  • Addiction medicine prescriber segmentation

Key findings to date:

  • [Populate from latest wave deliverable]

Why it matters: Sublocade ($856M, +13% YoY) is pulling away from Vivitrol ($468M, +2% YoY) in the OUD market. Sublocade's Feb 2025 label update (rapid induction + alternative injection sites) further strengthens its competitive position. This PET provides the only primary-source intelligence on how prescribers perceive the Vivitrol vs. Sublocade/Brixadi dynamic.

Risk:

  • Generic Vivitrol entry Jan 2027 reduces Alkermes' incentive to invest in branded promotional tracking
  • FY2026 is likely the last year of this engagement
  • Mitigation: Propose a "generic transition competitive study" as a successor engagement

SOV Tracker

3. ARISTADA SOV Tracker (Ongoing)

Status: 🟢 Active (institutional) SOW value: [Populate — ongoing contract] Scope: Quarterly waves, 125 HCPs per wave, 3-minute survey Signatory: Kurt Stauble (Sr. Dir., MR & CI) Running since: Pre-2021 (5+ years)

What it tracks:

  • Reach/frequency/SOV vs. Abilify Maintena/Asimtufii (Otsuka/Lundbeck), Invega franchise (J&J/Janssen), Uzedy/Risperdal Consta (Teva)
  • In-person vs. digital interaction mix
  • Regional variance in SOV
  • Prescriber segment analysis (high-decile vs. lower-decile HCPs)

Key findings (Q2'25, latest):

  • ARISTADA SOV stable at ~20% across all tracked quarters
  • Abilify (Maintena + Asimtufii) reached ~40% SOV — pulling away as leader
  • Combined Abilify reach ~66% of tracked HCPs
  • ARISTADA reach among Segment A (high-decile) HCPs declined in Q2'25
  • In-person interactions dominate (>80%) across all LAI brands
  • Regional variance: ARISTADA strongest in Northeast, weakest in Mid-Atlantic and South
  • ARISTADA call plan contracted from 27.4K to 9.3K NPIs — significant targeting contraction

Why it matters: The longest-running ZoomRx engagement on the account. Provides longitudinal SOV data that Alkermes uses for LAI sales force planning. The Q2'25 data showing Abilify pulling away and ARISTADA's call plan contracting confirms Alkermes is shifting investment to Lybalvi.

Watch items:

  • Call plan contraction may eventually lead Alkermes to reduce tracker scope or frequency
  • Uzedy's growth (Q1 2026: $63M, +62% YoY) and bipolar I approval may prompt Alkermes to request expanded tracking

Proposed / Pipeline

4. Sales Force Tactical Optimization ($89,500)

Status: ⚪ Proposed — awaiting client decision Proposed value: $89,500 Scope: Optimize detail delivery for SZ and BPD prescribing

Rationale: Complements the Lybalvi PET by moving from "what is the promotional landscape" (PET) to "how should Alkermes optimize its own promotional activity" (SFTO).

Decision timeline: [Populate — when was the proposal submitted? Is there a decision deadline?]

Cross-Workstream Themes

1. Cobenfy Intelligence Is the Strategic Currency

The Lybalvi PET's Cobenfy tracking is the single most valuable data stream on the account. Every strategic conversation with Alkermes DAI should reference Cobenfy competitive dynamics. This is what differentiates ZoomRx from any other PET vendor Alkermes could use.

2. The Account Is PET-Only — and That's a Ceiling

In 11 years, ZoomRx has only sold promotional effectiveness research to Alkermes. No HCPP, no ATU, no strategic consulting, no digital tracking. This constrains revenue growth and makes the relationship vulnerable to commoditization. Breaking into non-PET services is the account's most important strategic objective.

3. Vivitrol's Sunset Creates Both Risk and Opportunity

The Vivitrol PET will likely end after FY2026. This is ~$117K of revenue at risk. But the generic transition itself is a research opportunity — Alkermes will need intelligence on brand-to-generic switching dynamics, criminal justice channel loyalty, and AUD franchise protection.

4. LUMRYZ Is the White Space

Zero ZoomRx work on a $315-335M brand. The Avadel integration is in Year 1. The commercial team is being built. This is the most significant untapped opportunity on the account.

Immediate Priorities

  1. Confirm SFTO proposal status — has John Fritch responded?
  2. Ensure Lybalvi PET Q1 2025 wave (first with Cobenfy) delivers high-impact insights
  3. Map LUMRYZ commercial team contacts through Sara Farina
  4. Prepare Vivitrol generic transition research proposal for H2 2026
  5. Populate key findings sections from latest deliverables for all three active projects
  6. Confirm Aristada SOV Tracker annual contract value

Accounts Hub | Home
